# Low-jitter C2 beacon detection by inter-arrival coefficient-of-variation (T1071)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

The canonical beacon-cadence hunt: it uses `windowcomp lag(_time)` partitioned by (src, dst) to fetch each connection's previous timestamp, then `timestamp_diff(..., "SECOND")` to compute per-pair inter-arrival gaps. A `comp` collapses each pair to count, mean gap, and `stddev_population` (jitter), and the coefficient of variation `cv = divide(to_float(jitter), to_float(mean_gap))` classifies channels — tight beacons have CV near zero. Gotcha: `divide` needs `to_float` on both operands to avoid integer truncation, and `mean_gap > 0` guards against a divide-by-zero on single-burst pairs.

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.NETWORK and action_local_ip != null and action_remote_ip != null
| fields action_local_ip, action_remote_ip, _time
| windowcomp lag(_time) by action_local_ip, action_remote_ip sort asc _time as prev_time
| filter prev_time != null
| alter gap_s = timestamp_diff(_time, prev_time, "SECOND")
| comp count() as intervals, avg(gap_s) as mean_gap, stddev_population(gap_s) as jitter by action_local_ip, action_remote_ip
| filter intervals >= 4 and mean_gap > 0
| alter cv = divide(to_float(jitter), to_float(mean_gap))
| alter verdict = if(cv < 0.1, "tight-beacon", cv < 0.5, "regular", "irregular")
| sort asc cv
| limit 30
```
